mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in greater than 65% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.0%, it signals labor market deterioration and impending economic contraction, triggering risk-off sentiment that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses above the 4.0% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and stable or declining federal funds rates indicate robust consumer demand supported by accommodative financing conditions, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS rises but FEDFUNDS rises concurrently by more than 0.25%.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth signals expanding corporate capacity utilization and rising earnings momentum, which drives positive short-term S&P 500 returns during periods of stable inflation.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration coincides with negative 5-day returns in greater than 60% of observations during low-inflation periods.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects sustained economic expansion and wage growth, which supports corporate revenue forecasts and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no statistical correlation with 20-day forward returns with Rank IC below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index crosses above 305.0, it indicates entrenched inflationary pressures that force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, increasing equity risk premiums and causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL levels above 305.0 are followed by positive 60-day returns in greater than 50% of cases during tightening cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts combined with expanding industrial production indicate synchronized strength in both residential and non-residential sectors, boosting aggregate demand expectations and yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative returns when HOUST rises but INDPRO contracts by more than 0.5% month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Declining real GDP growth signals economic deceleration that reduces corporate profit expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter when the economy is already in a late-cycle phase.
failure_condition: GDPC1 declines are followed by positive 60-day returns in greater than 60% of late-cycle observations over a 5-year sample.
